examples.text: وَصَلِّ عَلَيْهِمْ إِنَّ صَلَاتَكَ سَكَنٌ لَهُمْ
examples.english: and pray for them—surely your prayer is a source of comfort for them.

examples.text: قُلِ ٱدْعُوا ٱللَّهَ أَوِ ٱدْعُوا ٱلرَّحْمَٰنَ أَيًّا مَا تَدْعُوا فَلَهُ ٱلْأَسْمَاءُ ٱلْحُسْنَى وَلَا تَجْهَرْ بِصَلَاتِكَ وَلَا تُخَافِتْ بِهَا وَٱبْتَغِ بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ سَبِيلًا
examples.english: Say (to them, O Mohammed): Call upon (Him by) "God" or call upon (Him by) "ar-Raḥmân" ("The Merciful One"); whichever you call upon (Him by), (remember that) the most beautiful of names belong to Him; and be not very loud in your prayers nor be very quiet but follow a way in between.

senses.gloss: prayer in general; spontaneous prayer; salat, namaz, prayer

examples.text: يُؤَدِّي اَلْمُسْلِمُونَ الصَّلَاةَ خَمْسَ مَرَّاتٍ فِي الْيَوْمِ
examples.english: Muslims perform the prayer five times a day.

senses.gloss: place of worship, especially of the Jews, hence a synagogue

examples.text: وَلَوْلَا دَفْعُ ٱللهِ ٱلنَّاسَ بَعْضَهُمْ بِبَعْضٍ لَّهُدِّمَتْ صَوَامِعُ وَبِيَعٌ وَّصَلَوَاتٌ وَّمَسٰجِدُ يُذْكَرُ فِيهَا ٱسْمُ ٱللهِ كَثِيرًا
examples.english: And were it not that God checks some people by others there in fact would have been demolished monastic cells and churches, and synagogues and mosques wherein the name of God is oft-mentioned.

senses.gloss: supplication, especially the supplication (from God) of blessing and well-being (on somebody)
supplication, especially the supplication (from God) of blessing and well-being (on somebody); blessing and well-being

examples.text: أُولَٰئِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ صَلَوَاتٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ وَرَحْمَةٌ
examples.english: Those are the ones upon whom are blessing and well-being as well as mercy from their Lord.

etymology.text: Normalized spelling of earlier صَلَوٰة (ṣalāh), from Classical Syriac ܨܠܘܬ (ṣəlōṯ), whence the denominal root ص ل و (ṣ l w). It is also thought to be derived from the root و ص ل (w ṣ l). Compare Ugaritic 𐎕𐎍𐎚 (ṣlt). Compare Biblical Hebrew צֶלָה (ṣélā).
